page: Amazon Web Services c6g.medium is a compute-optimized instance powered by a custom AWS Graviton processor using the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. It features 1 dedicated physical core and 1 vCPU running at 2.5 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 2.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Cache resources consist of 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core CPU capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1493.38. Due to the single-core limitation, multi-core performance matches the single-core score exactly. Memory bandwidth is relatively low, with read-only large block bandwidth at 19.65 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 9.83 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is supported by hardware-accelerated AES instructions, though other advanced instruction sets like AVX or AMX are absent.

From a resource density perspective, this instance offers an entry-level point into the ARM64 ecosystem on AWS. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require dedicated CPU resources but have low memory and storage demands. Tradeoffs include a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps and the lack of local NVMe storage. Appropriate workloads include lightweight single-threaded applications, basic static web serving, and simple task queue processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6g.medium is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance featuring a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 core running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 2.0 GB of RAM, 32 MB of L3 cache, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and bundled GPUs, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance but limited multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth due to its single-vCPU configuration. It supports hardware AES cryptography but lacks AVX and AMX instruction sets. This instance provides a cost-efficient entry point for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic static web serving, and simple task processing where dedicated CPU allocation is required without high memory or storage overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.medium is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 1 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-N1 core, 2.0 GB of RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.medium is a single-core ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 2.0 GB RAM and dedicated CPU allocation.
tagline: A single-core ARM64 compute instance with dedicated CPU allocation and low memory footprint for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 core running at 2.5 GHz
2.0 GB of system memory with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Average single-core CPU performance with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography
No local storage, zero baseline network bandwidth, and no bundled GPUs
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic static web serving
